content: How does the book of Mark begin its declaration about Jesus?
explanation: Mark 1:1 opens: 'The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.'

content: What did Jesus call Simon and Andrew to become?
explanation: Mark 1:17 — 'Follow me, and I will make you become fishers of men.'

content: What did Jesus say to the paralytic before healing his body?
explanation: Mark 2:5 — Before healing his body, Jesus said: 'Son, your sins are forgiven.'
options: Go wash in the pool of Siloam

content: When the storm arose on the sea, what did Jesus do?
explanation: Mark 4:39 — He rebuked the wind and said to the sea: 'Peace! Be still!'
options: Called the disciples to draw the nets
Rebuked the wind and said: Peace, be still!

content: How was the woman with the issue of blood healed?
explanation: Mark 5:27-29 — She touched Jesus' garment and was healed.

content: What did Jesus teach about little children when they were brought to Him?
explanation: Mark 10:14 — 'Let the children come to me... for to such belongs the kingdom of God.'
options: Children don't understand; wait until older
The kingdom of God belongs to those who are like little children

content: What amazed the people about Jesus' teaching at Capernaum?
explanation: Mark 1:22 — He taught as one who had authority, not as the scribes.

content: How did Jesus teach about the Sabbath?
explanation: Mark 2:27 — 'The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath.'
options: Man was made for the Sabbath
The Sabbath was made for man

content: What was the chief purpose Jesus appointed the twelve apostles for?
explanation: Mark 3:14 — That they might be with Him and that He might send them out to preach, having authority to cast out demons.
options: To be with Him and be sent out to preach
To keep the law of Moses

content: In the parable of the sower, what does the good soil represent?
explanation: Mark 4:20 — Good soil are those who hear the word, accept it, and bear fruit.
options: Hear the word, accept it, bear fruit

content: What did the demon-possessed man at Gerasa give as the demons' name?
explanation: Mark 5:9 — 'My name is Legion, for we are many.'

content: When feeding the five thousand, how many loaves and how many fish were there?
explanation: Mark 6:41 — Five loaves and two fish.

content: According to Jesus, what defiles a person?
explanation: Mark 7:20-23 — What comes out of the heart defiles a person.
options: What comes out of the heart

content: What is the condition for following Jesus according to Mark 8:34?
explanation: Mark 8:34 — Deny yourself, take up your cross daily, and follow Him.
options: Deny self, take up the cross, follow Him

content: What event occurred on the mountain with Peter, James, and John?
explanation: Mark 9:2-8 — Jesus was transfigured; Moses and Elijah appeared.

content: What did Jesus tell the rich young man to do for eternal life?
explanation: Mark 10:21 — Sell all you have and give to the poor; then come, follow Me.
options: Sell all his possessions for the poor and follow Him

content: Why did Jesus curse the fig tree?
explanation: Mark 11:13-14 — He found no fruit on it; so He pronounced a curse.

content: What is the greatest commandment according to Jesus in Mark 12?
explanation: Mark 12:29-31 — Love the LORD your God with all your heart... and love your neighbor as yourself.
options: Love the LORD and love your neighbor

content: About the day and hour of His return, what did Jesus teach?
explanation: Mark 13:32-37 — No one knows; stay awake and pray.
options: It will be known by signs in the sky
Angels will announce ahead of time

content: What did the Roman centurion say when he saw Jesus die?
explanation: Mark 15:39 — The centurion confessed Him as the Son of God.
options: This man is the king of the Jews
Truly this man was the Son of God!

content: Jesus said: 'Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are ___'
explanation: Mark 2:17 — Jesus came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.

content: Jesus said the Son of Man came not to be ___, but to ___
explanation: Mark 10:45 — A summary of Jesus' mission: to serve and give His life as a ransom for many.
options: Served / to serve and give His life as a ransom

content: Why did Jesus commend the widow who put in two small coins?
explanation: Mark 12:44 — The rich gave out of their abundance, but the widow gave all she had to live on. God looks at the heart and sacrifice.
options: She put in everything she had to live on

content: What was Jesus' first message at the start of His ministry?
explanation: Mark 1:15 — A summary of Jesus' entire message in one verse.
options: The time is fulfilled, the kingdom of God is at hand; repent and believe the gospel

content: What did Jesus say to calm the sea?
explanation: Mark 4:39 — Jesus has authority over nature. Wind and sea obey Him.

content: With how many loaves and fish did Jesus feed the 5,000?
explanation: Mark 6:41 — From five loaves and two fish, 5,000 ate to the full and twelve baskets remained.

content: What did Jesus say is the condition of following Him?
explanation: Mark 8:34 — Following Jesus requires giving up self-will and being willing to suffer for Him.
options: Deny yourself, take up your cross, and follow Me

content: The father seeking healing for his demon-possessed son said: 'I believe; help my ___'
explanation: Mark 9:24 — The most honest prayer: admit weak faith and ask God to help.

content: Jesus said we must receive the kingdom of God like whom?
explanation: Mark 10:15 — Children believe simply, without doubt. Faith requires simplicity and trust.

content: Mark 11:24: When you pray, believe that you have ___ and you will receive it
explanation: Praying with faith means believing God has heard and answered before seeing the result.

content: In Gethsemane, Jesus prayed: 'Abba, Father, take this cup from Me, yet not ___'
explanation: Mark 14:36 — Jesus submitted to the Father's will despite knowing the suffering ahead. A model prayer.

content: Jesus cried on the cross: 'Eloi, Eloi, lema sabachthani?' What language is this?
explanation: Mark 15:34 — A quote of Psalm 22:1. Jesus bore the sin of humanity and was forsaken by the Father.
options: Aramaic, meaning: My God, my God, why have You forsaken Me?

content: Jesus sent the disciples: 'Go into all the ___ and proclaim the gospel to every creature'
explanation: Mark 16:15 — The gospel is for all people, all places, all nations — not only the Jews.
